mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 130,000 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improved manufacturing activity, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO > 95.5 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining Fed funds rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a favorable macroeconomic environment for equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 2.5 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer and business demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with RSAFS and HOUST growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 9.0% signals tighter labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in UNRATE < 9.0 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) increases borrowing costs and reduces corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in FEDFUNDS > 2.0 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.
